proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative monotonic relationship across the ordinal scale).
Patients with kras_mutation=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than kras_mutation=0 patients.
Patients with braf_v600e=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than braf_v600e=0 patients.
Patients with msi_high=1 have different (longer) mean pfs_months than msi_high=0 patients.
Patients with her2_amplified=1 have different mean pfs_months than her2_amplified=0 patients.
Patients with right_sided_primary=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than right_sided_primary=0 patients.
treatment_regorafenib=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than treatment_regorafenib=0 patients (overall main effect).
treatment_cetuximab=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than treatment_cetuximab=0 patients (overall main effect).
treatment_bevacizumab=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than treatment_bevacizumab=0 patients (overall main effect).
treatment_pembrolizumab=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 patients (overall main effect).
treatment_encorafenib=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than treatment_encorafenib=0 patients (overall main effect).
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=0 patients (overall main effect).

analyses.result_summary: Stage IV patients have markedly shorter PFS: 3.703 mo (n=27,481) vs 5.055 mo (n=22,519); diff = -1.352 mo, p~0.
Each one-point increase in ECOG-PS associated with -1.19 months PFS; t = -83.99, p~0; rho = -0.36.
KRAS-mutant patients have shorter PFS: 4.121 vs 4.448 mo; diff = -0.327 mo, p = 1.86e-58.
BRAF V600E patients have shorter PFS: 4.094 vs 4.322 mo; diff = -0.228, p = 3.47e-07.
No detectable difference: 4.293 (MSI-H) vs 4.313 mo (MSS); diff = -0.019, p = 0.68.
No detectable difference: 4.258 vs 4.313 mo; diff = -0.056, p = 0.36.
Right-sided primaries have shorter PFS: 4.114 vs 4.419 mo; diff = -0.305, p = 1.04e-49.
Regorafenib-treated patients have substantially longer PFS overall: 5.089 vs 4.117 mo; diff = +0.972, p = 1.71e-216.
No main effect: 4.285 vs 4.323 mo; diff = -0.038, p = 0.088.
No main effect: 4.301 vs 4.320 mo; diff = -0.019, p = 0.354.
No main effect: 4.316 vs 4.311 mo; diff = +0.005, p = 0.856.
No main effect: 4.316 vs 4.311 mo; diff = +0.005, p = 0.873.
No main effect: 4.271 vs 4.315 mo; diff = -0.044, p = 0.244.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among RAS-wildtype patients (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0), treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_cetuximab=0; the cetuximab×RAS-status interaction is positive.
Among msi_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0; pembrolizumab×MSI interaction is positive.
Among braf_v600e=1 patients, treatment_encorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_encorafenib=0; encorafenib×BRAF interaction is positive.
Among her2_amplified=1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=0; the interaction is positive.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_cetuximab * ras_wt') and stratified Welch t-tests
analyses.result_summary: RAS-WT cetux ON 4.411 vs OFF 4.458 (diff -0.047, p=0.13); RAS-mut ON 4.127 vs OFF 4.158 (diff -0.030, p=0.32). Interaction term coef = -0.017, p=0.71. No biomarker-targeted benefit.
MSI-H pembro ON 4.299 (n=396) vs OFF 4.292 (n=2117), diff +0.007 (p=0.96). Interaction coef = +0.002, p=0.99. No MSI-H pembrolizumab effect.
BRAF V600E encora ON 3.979 (n=242) vs OFF 4.108 (n=2030), diff -0.129 (p=0.33). Interaction coef = -0.141, p=0.38. No BRAF-targeted encorafenib benefit.
HER2-amp trastu+tuca ON 4.273 (n=120) vs OFF 4.256 (n=1384), diff +0.017 (p=0.93). Interaction coef = +0.063, p=0.78. No HER2-targeted benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The +0.97-month regorafenib main effect on pfs_months persists after multivariable adjustment for prognostic covariates (age, ECOG, stage_iv, biomarkers, labs).
The regorafenib benefit on pfs_months differs between stage_iv=1 and stage_iv=0 (interaction).
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Inflammation markers crp_mg_l and nlr are inversely associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib + stage_iv + ecog_ps + age_years + sex_female + kras_mutation + braf_v600e + msi_high + right_sided_primary + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + cea_ng_ml')
analyses.result_summary: After multivariable adjustment, regorafenib coefficient remains around +0.94 mo (p~0); see iter 6 full model.
Stage IV regora benefit +1.011 (n=5552 vs 21929, p=4.4e-140); non-stage IV benefit +0.941 (n=4470 vs 18049, p=6.3e-95). Interaction coef = +0.070, p=0.151. Benefit is similar across stage strata.
Pearson r = +0.102, p = 3.25e-115. Higher albumin associates with longer PFS (in multivariable model coef = +0.469 per g/dL, p~0).
Pearson r = -0.123, p = 2.21e-167. More weight loss associates with shorter PFS.
Pearson r = -0.038, p = 3.13e-17. Higher CEA associates with shorter PFS (small but highly significant; MV coef -0.005 per ng/mL).
Pearson r = -0.011, p = 0.013 (univariate). After multivariable adjustment p=6.86e-19 with coef -0.0004/(U/L). Direction confirmed negative.
crp_mg_l: r = -0.008, p=0.065 (NS). nlr: r = +0.005, p=0.29 (NS). Neither shows a significant univariate association; in multivariable model crp p=0.055, nlr p=0.085.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation), after adjustment for ECOG and stage.
Mean pfs_months differs between sex_female=1 and sex_female=0.
Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories.
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured).
Mean pfs_months differs between rural_residence=1 and rural_residence=0.
Presence of liver_mets, bone_mets, adrenal_mets, pleural_effusion, pericardial_effusion, or contralateral_lung_mets is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher symptom-grade scores (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Comorbidities such as diabetes_mellitus, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, atrial_fibrillation, prior_malignancy, depression_anxiety_diagnosis are associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(age_years, pfs_months); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv')
analyses.result_summary: Univariate Pearson r = +0.766, p~0 (very strong). Adjusted for ECOG and stage, +0.176 months PFS per year of age, p~0. Older patients have systematically longer PFS in this cohort.
Female 4.308 vs male 4.314 mo; diff = -0.006, p=0.77. No sex difference.
ANOVA F p=0.479 across asian/black/hispanic/other/white. Means range 4.27-4.41 mo, no significant differences.
ANOVA F p=0.219; means medicaid 4.28, medicare 4.31, private 4.33, uninsured 4.25. No significant differences.
Rural 4.331 vs non-rural 4.307 mo; diff = +0.024, p=0.34. No effect.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests pfs_months ~ each metastasis indicator
analyses.result_summary: liver_mets diff -0.019 (p=0.52); bone_mets +0.031 (p=0.23); adrenal_mets +0.058 (p=0.089); pleural_effusion -0.052 (p=0.069); pericardial_effusion -0.079 (p=0.12); contralateral_lung_mets -0.046 (p=0.14). All non-significant; the metastatic-site flags are not prognostic in this cohort.

analyses.code: Spearman rho between each symptom-grade column and pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: fatigue_grade rho=-0.002, p=0.67; pain_nrs rho=-0.004, p=0.37; dyspnea_grade rho=-0.006, p=0.16; cough_grade rho=-0.004, p=0.33; appetite_loss_grade rho=+0.003, p=0.55. None significant.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests for each comorbidity vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: All p>0.05: diabetes p=0.61; hypertension p=0.46; copd p=0.81; CKD p=0.31; HF p=0.98; CAD p=0.92; AF p=0.12; VTE p=0.75; autoimmune p=0.42; prior_malignancy p=0.16; depression/anxiety p=0.054. No comorbidity is significantly prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 27 SNP columns shows a Bonferroni-significant association with pfs_months (alpha = 0.05/27 = 1.85e-3).
Greater prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Prior treatment exposures (chemotherapy, radiation, surgery, immunotherapy, targeted therapy) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
More years_since_diagnosis is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: for s in snps: spearmanr(df[s], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Smallest SNP p-values: snp_rs1801131 p=0.027 (rho=-0.010), snp_rs1050828 p=0.029 (rho=+0.010), snp_rs2070744 p=0.045. None pass Bonferroni alpha 1.85e-3. No SNP is reliably associated with PFS.
rho = +0.006, p = 0.154. No association.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests pfs_months ~ each prior_* indicator
analyses.result_summary: prior_chemotherapy diff -0.005 (p=0.82); prior_radiation +0.023 (p=0.30); prior_surgery -0.023 (p=0.28); prior_immunotherapy -0.034 (p=0.23); prior_targeted_therapy -0.030 (p=0.33). None significant.
r = +0.007, p = 0.142. No detectable association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model of pfs_months including age, sex, ECOG, stage, sidedness, key biomarkers (kras, nras, braf_v600e, msi_high, her2_amplified), labs (cea, albumin, ldh, weight loss, crp, nlr), and all six treatments, the significant prognostic predictors are age (+), albumin (+), regorafenib (+) and ECOG, stage_iv, right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, cea_ng_ml, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo (-).

analyses.result_summary: R^2 = 0.859. Significant (p<0.01): age_years +0.176, ecog_ps -1.171, stage_iv -1.361, right_sided_primary -0.287, kras_mutation -0.354, braf_v600e -0.357, cea_ng_ml -0.0050, albumin_g_dl +0.469, ldh_u_l -0.0004, weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.0761, treatment_regorafenib +0.943. Non-significant: sex_female, nras_mutation, msi_high, her2_amplified, crp_mg_l, nlr, cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab+tucatinib, liver_mets, bone_mets. The directions match canonical CRC prognostics except age (positive in this dataset) and the lack of a liver_mets effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is restricted to kras_mutation=0 patients; kras_mutation=1 patients derive no benefit (negative regorafenib×KRAS interaction).
The regorafenib benefit is absent or attenuated in braf_v600e=1 patients (negative regorafenib×BRAF interaction).
The regorafenib benefit is restricted to left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0); right-sided patients derive no benefit (negative regorafenib×right_sided interaction).
The regorafenib benefit is similar in magnitude in msi_high=1 and msi_high=0 patients (no regorafenib×MSI interaction).
The regorafenib benefit is similar across ecog_ps strata (0,1,2).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib * kras_mutation') and stratified Welch tests
analyses.result_summary: KRAS-WT: regora ON 5.773 vs OFF 4.117, diff = +1.656, p=6e-293. KRAS-mut: regora ON 4.143 vs OFF 4.116, diff = +0.027, p=0.46. Interaction coef = -1.629, p = 7.7e-226. Benefit confined to KRAS wildtype.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib * braf_v600e') and stratified Welch tests
analyses.result_summary: BRAF-WT: regora ON 5.144 vs OFF 4.116, diff = +1.027, p=5e-226. BRAF V600E: regora ON 3.971 vs OFF 4.126, diff = -0.156, p=0.13. Interaction coef = -1.183, p = 6.1e-23. Benefit absent (numerically reversed) in BRAF V600E.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib * right_sided_primary') and stratified Welch tests
analyses.result_summary: Left-sided: regora ON 5.601 vs OFF 4.121, diff = +1.480, p=4e-274. Right-sided: regora ON 4.135 vs OFF 4.109, diff = +0.026, p=0.52. Interaction coef = -1.454, p = 4.1e-168. Benefit confined to left-sided primaries.
MSS: regora benefit +0.973 (p=2.7e-206); MSI-H: regora benefit +0.951 (p=7.9e-12). Interaction coef = -0.022, p=0.85. No MSI-status interaction.

analyses.code: Stratified Welch tests within each ecog_ps level
analyses.result_summary: ECOG=0 diff +0.925 (p=5e-76); ECOG=1 +0.995 (p=4e-122); ECOG=2 +0.896 (p=3e-34). Benefit roughly constant across ECOG levels (no strong interaction).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the joint subgroup kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided RAS-WT), the regorafenib benefit on pfs_months exceeds 2 months and is the largest of any 2x2 KRAS×sidedness cell.

analyses.code: Stratified Welch tests across the 4 KRAS x sidedness cells
analyses.result_summary: KRAS=0/right=0 (left-sided RAS-WT-like): regora ON 6.639 (n=3763) vs OFF 4.119 (n=15090), diff = +2.520, p~0. The other three cells show no benefit: KRAS=0/right=1 +0.071 (p=0.16), KRAS=1/right=0 +0.061 (p=0.17), KRAS=1/right=1 -0.040 (p=0.52). Regorafenib benefit is concentrated almost entirely in left-sided KRAS-wildtype patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even in the canonical indication subgroup (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0), treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_cetuximab=0.
Within braf_v600e=1, the combination treatment_encorafenib=1 AND treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than other regimens.

analyses.code: Welch t-test in subgroup kras=0 & nras=0 & right_sided_primary=0
analyses.result_summary: RAS-WT left-sided cetuximab ON 4.574 (n=5510) vs OFF 4.635 (n=12414), diff = -0.062, p=0.13. Even in the canonical indication subgroup, no benefit detected.

analyses.code: Welch t-test in BRAF V600E subgroup: encora+cetux combo vs others
analyses.result_summary: Combo n=58 mean 4.151 vs other-in-V600E n=2214 mean 4.093, diff = +0.058, p=0.84. Sample is small but no signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After all probing, the dominant signals for pfs_months are: (1) older age (+), albumin (+), and treatment_regorafenib (+); (2) higher ECOG-PS (-), stage_iv (-), right_sided_primary (-), kras_mutation (-), braf_v600e (-), CEA (-), LDH (-), weight loss (-); (3) the regorafenib benefit is concentrated in KRAS-wildtype, BRAF-wildtype, left-sided patients; (4) cetuximab/pembrolizumab/encorafenib/trastuzumab+tucatinib show NO biomarker-targeted PFS benefit; (5) demographics, SNPs, comorbidities, mets sites and symptoms are not prognostic in this cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Summary hypothesis encapsulating findings; no new statistical test. See analysis_summary.txt for full narrative.
